question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the effect of various hormones on breast physiology?

options.A: Estrogen receptors are present only in breast cancer cells
options.B: Mammary ductal dilatation and differentiation of alveolar epithelial cells and secretory cells are the result of rising progesterone levels
options.C: The early first trimester breast changes are primarily due to the increased progesterone effects of pregnancy
options.D: Milk production and secretion after childbirth are maintained by ongoing secretion of prolactin by the anterior pituitary gland

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning local recurrence of breast cancer?

options.A: The percentage of patients with chest wall recurrence as their initial site of failure following mastectomy is similar for node-negative and node-positive patients
options.B: Most patients with local-regional recurrence of their disease will eventually die of metastatic disease
options.C: The treatment of local recurrence following mastectomy includes local radiation therapy and systemic chemotherapy
options.D: In-breast recurrence following breast conserving surgery is not a negative prognostic factor e.Regional lymph node recurrence following axillary node dissection is rare

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning non-invasive breast carcinoma?

options.A: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) is associated with a significant risk of development of invasive ductal carcinoma in the same quadrant of the same breast as the initial lesion
options.B: DCIS should not be treated with breast conservation therapy
options.C: Lobular carcinoma in situ (LCIS) is the most common form of non-invasive breast cancer
options.D: When LCIS is found, there is an up to 50% chance of lobular carcinoma in situ of the contralateral breast

question: The parathyroid glands:

options.A: Develop from the second and third pharyngeal pouches, along with the palatine tonsil and the thymus.
options.B: Migrate caudally in the neck in normal development but can be found anywhere from the pharyngeal mucosa to the deep mediastinum.
options.C: Secrete PTH and calcitonin to manage calcium homeostasis.
options.D: Usually number four, but frequently number only two or three.

question: True statements about pituitary anatomy and physiology include:

options.A: The pituitary has dual embryonic origin: the anterior pituitary arises from embryonic ectoderm; the posterior pituitary, from the diencephalon.
options.B: The hypophyseal portal system integrates function of the anterior and posterior pituitary.
options.C: Adrenocorticotropin (ACTH), formed by posttranslational processing of the precursor POMC, is normally controlled by hypothalamic CRF but may be released by immune-related mechanisms.
options.D: Growth hormone (GH) directly stimulates longitudinal growth of the skeleton and growth of muscles.

question: Hypercortisolism:

options.A: Is most often ACTH-dependent, owing to an ACTH-producing pituitary adenoma.
options.B: Is best diagnosed by measurement of cortisol from a serum sample collected at 8 A.M.
options.C: Is attributable to an adrenal source if the basal serum ACTH level is above 10 pg. per ml., if the hypercortisolism is suppressed by high-dose dexamethasone, and if an adrenal tumor is visualized radiographically.
options.D: May be caused by small cell carcinoma of the lung, carcinoid tumors, tumors of the endocrine pancreas, pheochromocytoma, or medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC).

question: Primary aldosteronism:

options.A: Produces a syndrome of diastolic hypertension, hypokalemia, and edema.
options.B: Is suggested by findings of serum potassium less than 3.5 mEq. per liter, urinary potassium excretion greater than 30 mEq. per day, upright plasma renin below 3 ng. per ml., and a plasma aldosterone concentration–plasma renin activity ratio greater than 20:1.
options.C: Is most often due to an aldosterone-producing adrenal adenoma, which may be distinguished from idiopathic adrenal hyperplasia by its sensitivity to diurnal changes in ACTH and insensitivity to changes in posture.
options.D: May be diagnosed in hypertensive patients by demonstration of an adrenal mass larger than 1 cm. on computed tomography alone.

question: Adrenocortical carcinoma:

options.A: May be suspected in a patient with rapidly progressive Cushing's syndrome and virilizing features or in asymptomatic patients with adrenal tumors larger than 6 cm on CT.
options.B: Most often is diagnosed early in its course when disease is confined to the adrenal gland.
options.C: Is differentiated from benign adrenocortical adenoma by tumor necrosis, hemorrhage, and cellular features of large hyperchromatic nuclei and more than 20 mitoses per high-power field.
options.D: Should be resected only if disease is localized to the adrenal gland; otherwise treatment with mitotane is indicated.

question: Addisonian crisis, or acute adrenocortical insufficiency:

options.A: Occurs only in patients with known adrenal insufficiency or in those receiving long-term supraphysiologic doses of exogenous steroids.
options.B: Can mimic an acute abdomen with fever, nausea and vomiting, abdominal pain, and hypotension.
options.C: May cause electrolyte abnormalities, including hypernatremia, hypokalemia, hypoglycemia, and hypercalcemia, as well as eosinophilia on peripheral blood smear.
options.D: Should be diagnosed with the rapid ACTH stimulation test before steroid replacement is instituted.

question: Incidental adrenal masses:

options.A: May be seen in as many as 10% of abdominal CT studies.
options.B: Most commonly represent pheochromocytoma; adrenocortical adenoma, adrenocortical carcinoma, and metastases from other primary cancers occur less frequently.
options.C: May represent adrenocortical carcinoma if greater than 6 cm. in diameter.
options.D: Should be routinely evaluated by measurement of 24-hour urine levels of catecholamines and their metabolites, cortisol, and aldosterone plus fine-needle aspiration.

question: Correct statements about thyroid function tests include which of the following?

options.A: Contraceptive pills and pregnancy increase the amount of thyroxin-binding globulin (TBG), and, consequently, the total T 4 level.
options.B: Anticonvulsive medications and chronic debilitating illnesses decrease the amount of TBG and, consequently, the total T 4 level.
options.C: Intravenous pyleography can lower the rate of active iodine uptake by the thyroid.
options.D: A triiodothyronine (T 3) suppression test that demonstrates nonsuppressibility of thyroid function is compatible with the diagnosis of Graves' disease, toxic adenoma, or functioning carcinoma.

question: 37.The types of thyroiditis that can cause abnormalities of surgical significance are which of the following?

options.A: Chronic lymphocytic thyroiditis (Hashimoto disease) b.Riedel struma
options.B: Inferior thyroid arteries c.Thyroidea ima arteries
options.C: Acute (viral) thyroiditis 38.The principal blood supply to the parathyroid glands is which of the following? a.Superior thyroid arteries
options.D: Parathyroid arterial branches directly from the external carotid artery e.Highly variable

question: 44.Hyperthyroidism results from all of the conditions noted below. Of the following which commonly require surgical management?

options.A: Graves’ disease b.Struma ovarii
options.B: Bilateral subtotal thyroidectomy
options.C: Unilateral total lobectomy on the side of dominant disease d.131I treatment
options.D: The majority of thyroid hormone in circulating plasma is bound to albumin, thus limiting the availability of the metabolically active form 47.The definitive treatment of choice for toxic multinodular goiter is? a.Total thyroidectomy

question: 62.A 25-year-old male has been taking 40 mg of prednisone PO qod for ulcerative colitis for 5 years. He undergoes an uneventful colectomy with endorectal pull-through and an ileoanal anastomosis. Which of the following statements regarding steriod management are correct?

options.A: On the day of surgery he should receive 100 mg hydrocortisone IV q6h
options.B: Computed tomography (CT) c.Arteriography
options.C: Prophylactic treatment with a somatostatin analogue will reduce the risk of postoperative pancreatitis d.Exogenous steroid replacement can be stopped after 3 months 63.Imaging of the adrenal gland is best achieved with which of the following techniques? a.Ultrasound
options.D: Scintigraphy with 131I-6 b-iodomethyl-19-norcholesterol (NP-59) e.Scintigraphy with 131I-methaiodobenzylguanidine (MIBG)

question: 66.Which of the following diagnostic tests will differentiate between the pituitary and adrenal gland as the cause of hypercortisolism?

options.A: High dose dexamethasone suppression test b.CRH stimulation test
options.C: The development of normal male external genitalia requires adrenal androgen production d.A male with congenital adrenal hyperplasia is likely to be feminized in appearance 68.Which of the following statements regarding the physiology of the adrenal gland are true? a.Release of CRH is regulated principally by negative feedback by ACTH
options.D: The plasma half-life of ACTH is relatively long (> 24 hrs) e.None of the above 69.The approximate 5-year survival rate for adrenocortical carcinoma is which of the following? a.0%

question: 70.Which of the following statements is true with respect to pheochromocytoma?

options.A: Pheochromocytoma associated with MEN IIa is usually unilateral and rarely malignant; therefore, unilateral exploration through a posterior flank incision is usually sufficient
options.B: Clonidine fails to suppress basal plasma-catecholamine levels in patients with pheochromocytoma c.NP-59 (131I-6 b-iodomethyl-19-norcholesterol) is taken up as cholesterol by the adrenal medulla
options.C: Primary hyperaldosteronism is characterized by hyperkalemia d.Secondary hyperaldosteronism occurs with renal artery stenosis
options.D: The ratio of plasma 3,4-dihydroxyphenoglycol (DHPG) to norepinephrine is generally elevated in patients with pheochromocytoma compared to patients with essential hypertension 71.Which of the following statements regarding aldosterone are true? a.Its secretion is directly related to the serum potassium concentration b.Angiotensin II is a more potent regulatory factor than ACTH

question: 72.A term neonate is noted to have ambiguous female genitalia. This infant is at risk for which of the following potentially life-threatening problems?

options.A: Cardiomyopathy with congestive heart failure b.Sodium wasting nephropathy with hypovolemia c.Respiratory failure from surfactant deficiency
options.B: The preferred surgical approach to the pituitary gland is via the sphenoid sinus
options.C: Growth hormone, ACTH, LH, FSH and serotonin are products of the adenohypophysis
options.D: Spontaneous hemorrhage from thrombocytopenia e.Pulmonary embolus from a hypercoaguable state 73.Which of the following adrenal lesions can be treated definitively by medical means? a.Benign functional adrenocortical adenoma

question: 75.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true with respect to growth hormone secreting pituitary adenomas?

options.A: Cerebral angiography b. Serum prolactin levels
options.B: Exogenous estrogen administration c.Diabetes mellitus
options.C: Magnetic resonance imaging of the brain d.Abdominal and pelvic CT scan 77.Which of the following condition(s) is/are associated with hyperprolactinemia? a.Chronic renal failure
options.D: Preoperative treatment of macroadenomas with a somatostatin analogue may improve postoperative remission rates 76.A 30-year-old woman presents with amenorrhea, headache and bitemporal hemianopsia. Appropriate diagnostic tests include:

question: 78.Pituitary adenomas are best classified according to functional hormone output. This information may be derived from which of the following?

options.A: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
options.B: The treatment of choice for hypercortisolism due to a pituitary adenoma in women of childbearing age is transsphenoidal total hypophysectomy
options.C: Patients who fail to remit with both surgery and radiation to the pituitary require either medical or surgical adrenalectomy
options.D: The long-term recurrence rate after resection of an ACTH-producing pituitary microadenoma is approximately 40%

question: Which of the following statements regarding unusual hernias is incorrect?

options.A: An obturator hernia may produce nerve compression diagnosed by a positive Howship-Romberg sign.
options.B: Grynfeltt's hernia appears through the superior lumbar triangle, whereas Petit's hernia occurs through the inferior lumbar triangle.
options.C: Sciatic hernias usually present with a painful groin mass below the inguinal ligament.
options.D: Littre's hernia is defined by a Meckel's diverticulum presenting as the sole component of the hernia sac.

question: Which of the following statements about the causes of inguinal hernia is correct?

options.A: Excessive hydroxyproline has been demonstrated in the aponeuroses of hernia patients.
options.B: Obliteration of the processus vaginalis is a contributing factor for the development of an indirect inguinal hernia.
options.C: Physical activity and athletics have been shown to have a protective effect toward the development of inguinal hernias.
options.D: Elevated levels of circulating serum elastalytic activity have been demonstrated in patients with direct herniation who smoke.

question: Which of the following congenital abnormalities are correctly defined?

options.A: Omphalocele represents a defect in the abdominal wall lateral to the umbilical cord.
options.B: The herniated viscera associated with omphaloceles are usually covered with a membranous sac.
options.C: An umbilical polyp is a small excrescence of omphalomesenteric duct mucosa that is retained in the umbilicus.
options.D: Meckel's diverticulum results when the intestinal end of the omphalomesenteric duct persists and represents a true diverticulum. Answer: BCD

question: 10.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the diagnosis and management of epigastric hernias?

options.A: The inferior epigastric artery and vein run upward in the preperitoneal fat posterior to the transversalis fascia close to the lateral margin of the internal inguinal ring
options.B: The iliohypogastric and ilioinguinal are motor and sensory nerves in the inguinal region which lie beneath the external oblique aponeurosis
options.C: The ilioinguinal nerve runs anterior to the spermatic cord in the inguinal canal and at the superficial inguinal ring, branches into the sensory supply to the pubic region and the upper scrotum or labium majoris
options.D: The genital branch of the genitofemoral nerve is a sensory nerve only to the upper thigh and genital area

question: 13.Chylous ascites is the accumulation of chyle within the peritoneal cavity. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning chylous ascites?

options.A: The best diagnostic test involves imaging of the abdominal wall by either CT or MRI b.Resection of the mass with a 2 cm margin is usually adequate
options.B: Either an abdominal or preperitoneal approach is possible c.The use of prosthetic mesh is required in all variations 15.A 28-year-old woman with a history of an appendectomy presents with a nontender palpable mass in the right lower quadrant abdominal incision. The following statement(s) is/are true concerning the diagnosis and management of this patient.
options.C: Low dose radiation is a suitable alternative to surgery for primary treatment
options.D: Treatment of chylous ascites with dietary manipulation will be successful in most cases e.The mortality rate in adults with chylous ascites is in excess of 50% 14.Which of the statement(s) is/are true concerning laparoscopic hernia repair? a.General anesthesia is required

question: 16.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning repair of inguinal hernias?

options.A: Most umbilical hernias in adults are the result of a congenital defect carried into adulthood b.A paraumbilical hernia typically occurs in multiparous females
options.B: Femoral and umbilical hernias are more common in women, with a female-to-male ratio of 4:1 c.The frequency of inguinal hernias increases with age 18.A 77-year-old multiparous female presents with a bowel obstruction. She has no previous abdominal operations and no abdominal wall hernias can be detected. In addition to her abdominal symptoms, she reports pain in her right medial thigh. The following statement(s) is/are true concerning her diagnosis and management.
options.C: The presence of ascites is a contraindication to elective umbilical hernia repair. d.Incarceration is uncommon with umbilical hernias
options.D: An advantage to the use of prosthetic material is the mesh incites formation of scar tissue to further increase tensile strength provided by the mesh alone 17.The following statement(s) is/are true concerning the epidemiology of inguinal hernias. a.Inguinal hernias occur with a male-to-female ratio of about 7:1

question: 20.Retroperitoneal fibrosis is a fibrosing condition of retroperitoneum, which is of significance as it generally encompasses the ureters and eventually causes hydronephrosis and kidney damage. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning this condition?

options.A: Simultaneous repair of bilateral direct inguinal hernias can be performed with no significant increased risk of recurrence
options.B: The preperitoneal approach may be appropriate for repair of a multiple recurrent hernia c.A femoral hernia repair can best be accomplished using a Bassini or Shouldice repair
options.C: The superficial inguinal ring d.The conjoined tendon 28.A number of special circumstances exist in the repair of inguinal hernias. The following statement(s) is/are correct.
options.D: Management of an incarcerated inguinal hernia with obstruction is best approached via laparotomy incision

question: Which of the following statements concerning intraperitoneal fluid collections are correct?

options.A: Ascites occurs when either the peritoneal fluid secretion rate increases or the absorption rate decreases.
options.B: Accumulation of lymph within the peritoneal cavity usually results from trauma as tumor involving the intra-abdominal lymphatic structures.
options.C: Choleperitoneum (intraperitoneal bile) generally occurs following biliary surgery, but spontaneous perforation of the bile duct has been reported.
options.D: The most common cause of hemoperitoneum is trauma to the liver or spleen. Answer: ABCD

question: Which of the following statements about esophageal anatomy is correct?

options.A: The esophagus has a poor blood supply, which is segmental in distribution and accounts for the high incidence of anastomotic leakage.
options.B: The esophageal serosa consists of a thin layer of fibroareolar tissue.
options.C: The esophagus has two distinct muscle layers, an outer, longitudinal one and an inner, circular one, which are striated in the upper third and smooth in the distal two thirds.
options.D: Injury to the recurrent laryngeal nerve results in vocal cord dysfunction but does not affect swallowing.

question: Which of the following statements about the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) mechanism, or high-pressure zone (HPZ), is true?

options.A: The LES is a circular smooth muscle ring that is 3 to 5 cm. long.
options.B: In assessing esophageal manometric data, mean HPZ pressure less than 6 mm. Hg or overall length less than 2 cm. is more likely to be associated with incompetence of the LES and gastroesophageal reflux.
options.C: Esophageal manometry and the acid perfusion (Bernstein) test reliably identify the patient with an incompetent LES mechanism.
options.D: Distal HPZ relaxation occurs within 5 to 8 seconds of initiating a swallow.

question: Which of the following statements about esophageal motility is/are true?

options.A: The act of swallowing initiates UES relaxation, which persists until the bolus of food passes the LES.
options.B: The primary peristaltic wave normally propels the swallowed bolus through the esophagus in 4 to 8 seconds.
options.C: Normally, a progressive peristaltic contraction (primary wave) follows 50% of all swallows, the remainder being secondary or tertiary contractions.
options.D: Secondary peristalsis is initiated when the entire swallowed bolus of food fails to empty from the esophagus into the stomach.

question: Which of the following statements about pathology encountered at esophagoscopy is/are correct?

options.A: Reflux esophagitis should be graded as mild, moderate, or severe, to promote consistency among different observers.
options.B: An esophageal reflux stricture with a 2-mm. lumen is not dilatable and is best treated with resection.
options.C: A newly diagnosed radiographic distal esophageal stricture warrants dilation and antireflux medical therapy.
options.D: In patients with Barrett's mucosa, the squamocolumnar epithelial junction occurs 3 cm. or more proximal to the anatomic esophagogastric junction.

question: All of the following statements about surgical management of gastric lymphomas are true except:

options.A: Stage I gastric lymphomas (small lesions confined to the stomach wall) can be cured completely with surgical therapy alone.
options.B: Extensive gastric lymphomas that initially are treated with radiation and/or chemotherapy occasionally perforate during treatment and require secondary resection.
options.C: Patients explored with a presumptive diagnosis of gastric lymphoma should undergo an attempt at curative resection when this is safe and feasible.
options.D: Without a preoperative diagnosis resection for gastric mass should not be attempted unless lymphoma can be excluded.

question: Which of the following statements about the anatomic basis for the syndrome of vascular compression of the duodenum are true?

options.A: The duodenum is obstructed in its distal third as it crosses over the lumbar vertebral column.
options.B: Structures crossing beneath the superior mesenteric artery include the duodenum, the uncinate process of the pancreas, and the left renal vein.
options.C: Hyperextension of the back allows the angle of origin of the superior mesenteric artery to widen, lessening the obstruction of the duodenum.
options.D: Patients are at significant risk for vascular compression of the duodenum if the angle between the takeoff of the superior mesenteric artery and the aorta is less than 45 degrees.

question: A 34-year-old morbidly obese diabetic woman underwent a gastric bypass about 12 hours ago. The operation was technically difficult but finally went well. You are called because she now has a temperature of 99.2؛ F, pulse of 134, and some pain in her incision and her back. She looks well; the incision is clean; and her examination is otherwise negative. A bolus of 500 ml. of dextrose/lactated Ringer's did not change her vital signs, except that her pulse rose to 140 without an increase in urine output. Your next step should be:

options.A: Another bolus of crystalloids.
options.B: Posterioanterior and lateral chest films.
options.C: Obtain white cell count, differential count, and electrolyte values.
options.D: Call the operating room and warn them that you need to re-explore for a leak. E. Increase her pain medication.

question: Which of the following statement(s) concerning the surgical options for an anti-reflux operation is/are true?

options.A: A patient with normal esophageal length and esophageal body motility is best served by laparoscopic Nissen fundoplication
options.B: A patient with a low peristaltic amplitude of the distal third of the esophagus is a candidate for an open Nissen fundoplication
options.C: A Collis gastroplasty is an additional procedure that can be added in patients with extensive esophageal shortening
options.D: End-stage reflux disease such as an undilatable stricture or Barrett’s esophagus with high grade dysplagia is best managed by a colon interposition

question: Fundamental to understanding disorders of esophageal function is the measurement of the contractility of the esophageal body and sphincters. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning esophageal manometry in the investigation of benign esophageal disease?

options.A: A defective sphincter is predictive of poor long-term response to medical therapy, but a good response to surgery
options.B: Esophageal manometry can determine the resting pressure and the overall length of the sphincter but not its abdominal length
options.C: The LES pressure normally drops to gastric baseline immediately after a swallow before the peristaltic wave reaches the lower esophagus
options.D: A Vector Volume below the fifth percentile of normal is the most sensitive measure of mechanical deficiency of the LES

question: The results for anti-reflux surgery are generally good, however, patients who have failed anti-reflux procedures constitute a particularly challenging group. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning failed anti-reflux repairs?

options.A: A Slipped Nissen is usually the result of an operative technical mistake
options.B: Disruption of a fundoplication is more prone to occur with a Nissen fundoplication because of the use of the gastric wall in the repair
options.C: Postoperative dysphagia in a patient with normal preoperative motility is usually due to a secondary motility disorder
options.D: Colonic replacement, although technically challenging, usually has superior long-term results when compared to esophageal replacement with the stomach

question: Which of the following patient scenarios would be best managed with anti-reflux surgery?

options.A: A patient with heartburn but normal 24 hour pH monitoring and an intact lower esophageal sphincter
options.B: A patient with primarily respiratory manifestations of gastroesophageal reflux
options.C: A patient with increased acid exposure and a mechanically defective sphincter who responds well to medical therapy but requires continued long-term medication for continued relief
options.D: A patient with gastroesophageal reflux but excessive complaints of epigastric pain, nausea, vomiting, and loss of appetite

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the blood supply and lymphatic drainage of the esophagus?

options.A: The thoracic esophagus receives no direct branches from the aorta therefore allowing the technique of transhiatal (blunt) esophagectomy
options.B: Bleeding esophageal varices are most prominent in the mid-esophagus
options.C: Lymphatic drainage of the lower third of the esophagus goes entirely to the abdominal lymphatic system
options.D: Nodal involvement in esophageal cancer is quite common even if the tumor is limited to the level of the submucosa

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the pathology of squamous cell carcinoma of the esophagus?

options.A: Carcinoma in situ will gradually progress to invasive squamous cell carcinoma over a period of two to four years
options.B: The most common location for squamous cell carcinoma of the esophagus is the upper and mid-thoracic segment
options.C: Esophageal carcinoma tends to be multifocal
options.D: Macroscopically, ulcerative lesions with extensive infiltration of the adjacent esophageal wall are most common

question: A 54-year-old woman experiences pain in both the anterior and posterior left chest and the epigastrium following balloon dilatation performed for achalasia. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning this patient’s diagnosis and management?

options.A: A normal chest x-ray will rule out an esophageal perforation
options.B: Barium should never be used in performance of a contrast study with a diagnosis of esophageal perforation
options.C: Conservative, nonoperative treatment may be indicated
options.D: If surgical repair is necessary, the patient should undergo esophagomyotomy and a partial gastric fundoplication

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct concerning the options for resection of esophageal carcinoma?

options.A: The development of reflux esophagitis seldom occurs following intrathoracic resection due to the limited life expectancy of these patients
options.B: Transhiatal esophagectomy, although conceptually sound, is not technically possible in most patients with esophageal carcinoma
options.C: Transhiatal resection, although less morbid, has unfavorable survival statistics compared to transthoracic resection
options.D: Radical transthoracic esophagectomy with en bloc dissection of continuous lymph node bearing tissues has not been shown to improve survival over transhiatal esophagectomy

question: As a meal is emptied from the stomach, gastric acid secretion gradually returns to baseline. Which of the following statements correctly characterize control of gastric acid secretion?

options.A: In humans, the most important inhibitory influence on gastrin release is exposure of the gastric mucosa to luminal acid
options.B: Acidification of the antral lumen causes reciprocal increases in somatostatin release and decreases in gastrin secretion
options.C: Antral distension stimulates gastric acid secretion
options.D: Acidification of the duodenal bulb inhibits gastric acid secretion

question: Helicoabacter pylori has been investigated as a possible etiologic agent in duodenal ulceration. Which of the following statement(s) regarding H pylori infection in humans is/are correct?

options.A: H pylori may be isolated from antral gastric mucosa in nearly 100% of patients with active duodenal ulceration but only 1–2% of normal volunteers
options.B: H pylori possess cell surface receptors that bind to small intestinal mucous cells
options.C: Therapeutic regimens for duodenal ulcer that eliminate the organism are associated with lower ulcer recurrence rates than those in which the organism persists
options.D: The incidence of the organism in the normal population increases with age e. Antral gastritis is associated with development of duodenal ulcer

question: 101.A 42-year-old man with a recently diagnosed duodenal ulcer develops melena and near-syncope. After fluid resuscitation, upper gastrointestinal endoscopy is performed. During the examination, a 1 cm ulcer is noted in the proximal duodenum. A fresh clot is observed within the ulcer and blood is noted to be oozing around the clot. Optimal therapy would consist of which of the following?

options.A: Omental patch of the perforation followed by truncal vagotomy and antrectomy after 8 weeks b. Omental patch of the perforation followed by truncal vagotomy and pyloroplasty after 8 weeks c.Omental patch of the perforation followed by chronic cimetidine administration
options.B: Irrigation of the clot followed by endoscopic application of a heat probe c.Transfusion and intravenous cimetidine
options.C: Conversion of Billroth II gastrojejunostomy to Billroth I gastroduodenostomy d. Conversion of Billroth II gastrojejunostomy to Roux-en-Y gastrojejunostomy e. Roux-en-Y hepaticojejunostomy 103.A 50-year-old male with a 2 year history of duodenal ulceration develops sudden, severe epigastric pain 4 hours prior to evaluation. Physical examination reveals T 101° F, pulse 80, BP 125/90, diminished bowel sounds, and abdominal muscular rigidity. An upright chest x-ray reveals pneumoperitoneum. At laparotomy, an anterior perforation in the first portion of the duodenum is observed. Optimal treatment would include:
options.D: Omental patch of the perforation plus proximal gastric vagotomy e. Omental patch of the perforation only

question: 114.Which of the following statements regarding gastroplasty and gastric bypass for morbid obesity is/are correct?

options.A: Coronary artery disease b. Hypertension
options.B: Gastric bypass is followed by progressive weight loss over a period of 36 months c.Gastric bypass is associated with a ―failure‖ of weight loss in 10 to 15% of patients
options.C: Adult-onset diabetes mellitus
options.D: Obesity hypoventilation and sleep apnea e. Pulmonary embolization

question: 120.A patient with gastric adenocarcinoma undergoes subtotal gastrectomy. Pathological examination reveals that the tumor penetrates to the serosa. Regional lymph nodes are not involved. Distant metastases are not detected. What is the correct tumor stage and 5-year survival rate?

options.A: Produce the brush border appearance. B. Contain amylase.
options.B: Amylase has 1-4 straight chains and 1-6 side chains. C. Amylase breaks 1-4 glucose linkages.
options.C: Contain dissacharidases. D. Increase absorptive area.
options.D: Amylase breaks 1-6 side chains.

question: 7.Which of these statements about the digestion of fat are true?

options.A: Micellar solution provides an optimal environment for the action of pancreatic lipase.
options.B: Inducing vomiting.
options.C: Decreasing intestinal absorption of water.
options.D: Causing secretion of water into the intestinal lumen. E. Causing edema of the intestinal wall. Answer: ABCDE

question: 9.History and physical examination permit the diagnosis of intestinal obstruction in most cases. Which of the following are important for the clinical diagnosis of small bowel obstruction?

options.A: Gastric hyperacidity and hypersecretion. B. Hyperoxaluria.
options.B: The number of portals. C. The number of fractions.
options.C: Hypermetabolic response.
options.D: The total amount of irradiation. E. All of the above.

question: 19.In addition to its absorptive and digestive roles, the small bowel also plays a significant role in the body’s immune system. Gut-associated lymphoid tissue (GALT) represents a major division of the immune system. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the immunologic functions of the small intestine?

options.A: The B lymphocytes of the small intestine do not produce immunoglobulin A (IgA)
options.B: Peyer’s patches, an example of an aggregated cellular portion of the gut-associated lymphoid system tissue, are large collections of lymphoid follicles found on the antimesenteric border of the ileum
options.C: The major immunoglobulin of the intestinal immune system is IgM
options.D: IgA produced by the intestinal immune system produces the classic Fc-mediated inflammatory reactions to antigen stimulus

question: 20.During the fasting state, a well-defined pattern of small bowel electrical activity occurs which is known as the interdigestive myoelectric complex or the migrating motor complex (MMC). Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the MMC?

options.A: This complex consists of a cyclic pattern of spike bursts and muscular contractions that migrate from the duodenum to the terminal ileum and can be divided into four phases
options.B: The major activity during the MMC occurs during phase I
options.C: In humans the MMC usually lasts less than one hour
options.D: Blood levels of the GI peptide, motilin, correlate closely with MMC activity and exogenous motilin can induce the MMC front

question: 21.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the anatomy of the small intestine?

options.A: The second (descending), third (transverse) and fourth (ascending) portions of the duodenum lie in the retroperitoneum and are mobilized for surgical procedures via the Kocher maneuver
options.B: The identification of the superior mesenteric vein and artery can be facilitated by an extensive Kocher maneuver mobilizing the transverse portion of the duodenum and exposing the vessels as they course over the duodenum and under the neck of the pancreas
options.C: In only the minority of patients can the accessory pancreatic duct (the duct of Santorini) be seen on endoscopic exam entering the duodenum
options.D: The ileum is the widest portion of the small intestine, with the diameter of the small bowel progressively increasing as the ileocecal valve is approached

question: 22.Historically, the small intestine was presumed to have only digestive and absorptive function. However, in the last decade the small intestine has become recognized as the body’s largest endocrine organ, producing a number of hormones, neurotransmitters, and paracrine substances. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning small bowel hormones?

options.A: Cholecystokinin (CCK) is produced from cells in the mucosa of the duodenum and jejunum and is released in response to luminal fats and proteins
options.B: Secretin is released in response to rising intraduodenal pH, resulting in inhibition of pancreatic secretion
options.C: Motilin is a 22-amino acid peptide released during the fasting state with increased levels corresponding with the onset of the migrating motor complex (MMC)
options.D: Neurotensin is produced primarily in the duodenal mucosa and its release is stimulated primarily by carbohydrates and proteins

question: 23.The enterohepatic circulation refers to the circular flow of bile through the small intestine and liver. Which of the following statement(s) concerning the absorption of bile salts is/are correct?

options.A: The enterohepatic circulation is highly efficient with 80% to 90% of secreted bile salts reabsorbed and returned to the liver through the portal circulation
options.B: The reabsorption of bile is entirely an active process
options.C: The small amount of bile escaping in the colon is deconjugated by bacteria, promoting lipid solubility and passive colonic absorption
options.D: Ileal resection results in presenting high concentrations of bile salts to the colon which promotes diarrhea by bacterial overgrowth

question: 25.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the pathophysiology of small bowel obstruction?

options.A: Simple obstruction secondary to an adhesion is most likely to resolve nonoperatively b.It is most likely that the patient’s obstruction is secondary to recurrent malignancy
options.B: Elevation of luminal pressure contributes to fluid accumulation in the small bowel in closed loop but not open loop small bowel obstructions
options.C: A history of colon cancer makes carcinomatosis the most likely diagnosis
options.D: Lower abdominal procedures are more likely to result in obstructive adhesions than are upper abdominal procedures

question: 32.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning postoperative ileus?

options.A: Urgent laparotomy because of the massive colon distension
options.B: An attempt at endoscopic decompression with a flexible sigmoidoscope
options.C: Elective laparotomy and sigmoid resection should follow if endoscopic decompression is successful
options.D: Contrast radiographic studies have no role in distinguishing early postoperative bowel obstruction from normal ileus 33.The initial management of this patient should consist of:

question: 35.A common manifestation of Crohn’s disease is perianal disease, including anal fistulas with extension to adjacent organs and soft tissue regions, fissures, and perirectal abscesses. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning perianal disease with Crohn’s disease?

options.A: Perianal disease is the initial mode of presentation in the majority of patients
options.B: Cholelithiasis c.Arthritis
options.C: Metronidazole has been shown to be effective in the treatment of perianal disease secondary to Crohn’s
options.D: An aggressive surgical approach is appropriate in most cases due to the frequent rapid progression of perianal disease 36.Nongastrointestinal complications of Crohn’s disease include: a.Renal calculi

question: 37.Which of the following points is/are true concerning the diagnosis of Crohn’s disease?

options.A: The leading infectious agent thus far suggested is infection with a Mycobacterium species b.Strong evidence linking viral pathogens to Crohn’s disease has been developed
options.B: Frozen section examination of the margin of resection is essential to prevent both recurrent disease and early anastomotic complications
options.C: Although many alterations in cellular and immune functions in patients with Crohn’s disease have been observed, no primary defect in the immune system has yet been identified
options.D: The identification of antibodies to enterocytes provides strong support for the theory that Crohn’s disease is an autoimmune process

question: 40.Crohn’s disease is an incurable disease, therefore recurrence after surgical resection is likely. Which of the following statement(s) regarding the recurrence of Crohn’s disease is/are accurate?

options.A: Endoscopic evidence of recurrence is present in less than 50% of patients at five years
options.B: Radiographic or endoscopic evidence of recurrence is frequently not accompanied by symptoms
options.C: Clinical recurrence of Crohn’s disease is seen in 20% of patients at two years, and 40–50% at four years after surgery
options.D: Reoperation for Crohn’s disease is necessary in the majority of patients by five years

question: 41.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning drug therapy for Crohn’s disease?

options.A: Crohn’s disease has an age distribution with peaks between the ages of 15 and 30 years and 65 and 75 years
options.B: There is a definite female predilection for Crohn’s disease
options.C: The disease is equally prevalent in industrialized versus underdeveloped countries
options.D: First and second generation relatives with Crohn’s disease have an increased prevalence when compared to the general population

question: 44.The management of adenocarcinoma of the small intestine depends primarily on tumor location. Which of the following statements concerning surgical management are true?

options.A: Radical pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple resection) is necessary for resection of most duodenal adenocarcinomas
options.B: Adenocarcinomas of the jejunum or ileum are managed by limited segmental resection including resection of the mesentery down to the first vascular arcade
options.C: Distal ileal carcinomas are best managed by right hemi-colectomy to include lymph node chains along the ileo-cecal blood supply
options.D: Small invasive adenocarcinomas of the ampulla and peri-ampullary duodenum can frequently be managed by local excision

question: Primary gastrointestinal lymphomas involving the small bowel are uncommon accounting for less than 5% of all lymphomas. Conditions associated with small intestinal lymphomas include which of the following?

options.A: Occurs after operations for ileal Crohn's but not colonic Crohn's. B. Is usually found just proximal to an enteric anastomosis.
options.B: In excising part of the colon containing cancer, the lymphatics should be avoided by dividing the mesentery close to the wall of the colon.
options.C: Despite complete removal of the colon and rectum, transanal fecal flow can be preserved by means of an ileal pouch–anal anastomosis.
options.D: When a colostomy is created it cannot be reversed.

question: 10.Which of the following statements about colon physiology is/are correct?

options.A: Colonic recycling of urea is accomplished by the splitting of urea by bacterial ureases. B. Fermentation by colonic bacteria may rescue malabsorbed carbohydrates.
options.B: ―Antiperistaltic‖ contractions occur in the descending colon. C. The rectum can accommodate stool by receptive relaxation. D. Stool in the colon is propelled by tonic contractions.
options.C: A negative osmotic gap in stool is indicative of secretory diarrhea.
options.D: A colonic transit time study involves serial abdominal x-rays after ingestion of radiopaque markers. E. Carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA) is useful for monitoring patients after resection for colon cancer. Answer: BCDE

question: 13.Which of the following statements about anorectal functional testing is/are true?

options.A: Anorectal manometry is often performed through open-tipped multilumen catheters perfused with fluid.
options.B: The predominant bacteria in the colon are aerobic.
options.C: Nearly one third of the dry weight of feces is bacteria.
options.D: Common bacteria in the colon are Bacteroides, Bifidobacterium, and Enterobacterium species.

question: 15.Which of the following statements about bowel preparation for colon surgery is/are true?

options.A: Bowel preparation is accomplished by a combination of mechanical cleansing and nonabsorbable antibiotics.
options.B: Three days of clear liquids provides sufficient mechanical cleansing.
options.C: Commercial electrolyte-polyethylene glycol solutions provide mechanical cleansing without inducing electrolyte imbalance.
options.D: Nonabsorbable antibiotics such as neomycin and erythromycin base are administered the day before the operation in three doses.

question: 16.Which of the following patients generally does not require surgical intervention as a consequence of acute diverticulitis?

options.A: It is more common in the United States and Western Europe than in Asia and Africa. B. A low-fiber diet may predispose to development of diverticulosis.
options.B: Colonic obstruction. C. Colovesical fistula. D. Free perforation.
options.C: It involves sigmoid colon in more than 90% of patients.
options.D: Sixty per cent develop diverticulitis sometime during their lifetime. 19.The most common indication for surgery secondary to acute diverticulitis is: A. Abscess.

question: 20.Which of the following is/are true about colorectal polyps?

options.A: Familial juvenile polyposis is associated with an increased incidence of colon cancer.
options.B: Although the propensity for development of malignancy is related to the size of a neoplastic polyp, those with mixed tubulovillous histologic appearance are most likely to develop malignant changes.
options.C: The loss of a single tumor suppressor gene such as p53 is sufficient to lead to the development of malignancy in colorectal neoplastic polyps.
options.D: Endoscopic polypectomy results in a decreased incidence of carcinomas of the colon and rectum. Answer: AD

question: 21.Which of the following statements about familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP) is/are true?

options.A: A history of abdominal operation. B. A mobile cecum.
options.B: The disorder is more common in men than in women.
options.C: Continence nearly always is recovered after correction of the prolapse. D. All of the above are true.
options.D: Chemotherapy. 31.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true about the maintenance of continence? A. It depends on both the internal and external sphincters as well as the puborectalis.

question: 33.Which of the following statements about hemorrhoids is/are not true?

options.A: Surgical resection.
options.B: 400 ml c.600 ml d.800 ml e.1000 ml
options.C: Calcitonin gene-related peptide d.Bradykinin 38.How much of the daily insensible water loss is due to loss in stool? a.200 ml
options.D: The treatment is surgical. E. All of the above. 36.Which of the following statements regarding the vasculature of the colon and rectum is/are correct? a.The middle colic artery is a branch of the superior mesenteric artery

question: 39.A 52-year-old woman is involved in an automobile accident and sustains an open fracture of the fight femur, compression fractures of the 10th and 11th thoracic vertebrae and right pulmonary contusion. On the fourth day after injury, her abdomen is noted to be distended, tympanitic and diffusely tender. Abdominal radiographs reveal gaseous distension of the ascending and transverse segments of the colon. The cecum is 13 cm in greatest diameter. Appropriate management includes which of the following as the next step?

options.A: Right hemicolectomy b.Operative cecostomy c.Colonoscopy
options.B: Total bowel transit time of 24 hours c.Total bowel transit time of 48 hours d.Total bowel transit time of 96 hours e.Marfinoid habitus 41.Which of the following statements regarding the myenteric plexus of the colon is/are correct? a.The myenteric plexus is located between the longitudinal and circular layers of the bowel wall b.The myenteric plexus contains only sensory neurons
options.C: The density of neurons with the colonic plexuses decreases along the length of the bowel d.Neurons of the myenteric plexus control the motor function of the colon
options.D: Contrast enema e.Observation 40.Which of the following features is/are consistent with a diagnosis of colonic inertia? a.Alternating episodes of severe constipation and normal bowel activity

question: Bile formation is:

options.A: An active secretory process.
options.B: Determined at two sites principally.
options.C: Regulated physiologically by hormones.
options.D: Largely determined by the intactness of the enterohepatic circulation (EHC). Answer: ABCD

question: Which of the following statements about the peritoneovenous shunt (PVS) is/are correct?

options.A: For cirrhotic patients with intractable ascites, the LeVeen shunt is an effective ―bridge‖ to liver transplantation.
options.B: Replacement of ascites with saline or lactated Ringer's solution reduces the coagulopathy following PVS.
options.C: For patients with cirrhotic ascites, the survival using repeated paracentesis with 5% albumin infusion is equivalent to that with the PVS.
options.D: Oliguria (less than 25 ml. per hour) in the immediate postoperative period following PVS should be treated with a 5% albumin infusion.

question: Which of the following clinical situations are considered good indications for PVS?

options.A: A 50-year-old cirrhotic man had an emergency portacaval shunt for bleeding varices and postoperatively had an ascites leak and mild superficial wound infection.
options.B: A 57-year-old woman with primary biliary cirrhosis (PBC) has difficult to control ascites and diuretic-induced encephalopathy.
options.C: A 46-year-old resistant alcoholic has chronic ascites uncontrolled by diuretics combined with repeat paracentesis.
options.D: A 34-year-old woman taking BCPs had rapid onset of ascites and is found to have hepatic vein thrombosis causing the Budd-Chiari syndrome.

question: Which of the following explanations account(s) for the fact that hepatitis C is the most common cause of posttransfusion hepatitis?

options.A: There are more carriers of hepatitis C virus (HCV) in the normal population who serve as blood donors.
options.B: Blood infected with hepatitis B virus (HBV) is eliminated through routine testing, leaving only HCV as the other blood-borne pathogen.
options.C: Current serologic tests for HCV antigen do not exclude carriers.
options.D: Questions designed to eliminate risk groups for HCV from the normal donor population may not be as specific as would be desirable.

question: Which of the following statements about choledocholithiasis are correct?

options.A: Common duct stones can originate in the gallbladder and migrate to the common duct, and stones can form de novo in the duct system.
options.B: Calcium bilirubinate stones are associated with the presence of bacteria in the duct system.
options.C: Common duct stones discovered at laparoscopic cholecystectomy should be treated by postoperative endoscopic extraction.
options.D: The serum bilirubin value is usually greater than 15 mg. per dl. in the patient with a symptomatic common duct stone. Answer: ABC

question: A benign biliary duct stricture:

options.A: Need not be treated unless it causes clinical jaundice.
options.B: Should always be treated by percutaneous balloon drainage.
options.C: Is prone to recur after treatment with biliary-enteric anastomosis.
options.D: When due to chronic pancreatitis should be treated by side-to-side choledochoduodenostomy. Answer: CD

question: Both endocrine and exocrine tissue comprise the pancreas. Which statement(s) is/are true?

options.A: The islets of Langerhans total 1 million per gland and drain their secretions via intercalated duct cells through the ampulla of Vater.
options.B: Islet alpha cells produce glucagon.
options.C: Islet sigma cells produce somatostatin.
options.D: The acini and ductal systems constitute the exocrine portion of the pancreas. Answer: BD

question: Pancreatic exocrine secretory products include a bicarbonate-rich electrolyte solution as well as digestive enzymes. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true?

options.A: Cholecystokinin (CCK) is the most potent endogenous stimulant of pancreatic enzyme secretion.
options.B: The chloride and bicarbonate concentrations of pancreatic juice vary and depend on the secretory flow rate.
options.C: Secretin is the most potent endogenous stimulant of pancreatic water and electrolyte secretion.
options.D: The peptidases synthesized by acinar cells are released into the pancreatic duct system in active form. Answer: ABC

question: Which of the following statements about pancreatic ascites is/are correct?

options.A: Patients typically present with painful ascites, reflecting the release of toxic pancreatic enzymes into the peritoneal cavity.
options.B: The standard evaluation of a patient with new-onset ascites includes abdominal paracentesis. In cases of pancreatic ascites, the peritoneal fluid contains high concentrations of both amylase and protein.
options.C: Pancreatic ascites can follow an episode of acute pancreatitis.
options.D: Patients with pancreatic ascites may fail to improve with nonoperative therapy and require surgical procedures. At abdominal exploration an acceptable approach to the pancreatic duct disruption involves suture ligation with omental patching. Answer: BC

question: Which of the following statements about gastrinoma (Zollinger-Ellison syndrome) is/are correct?

options.A: As many as 75% of gastrinoma patients have sporadic disease; 25% have gastrinoma associated with multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN 1).
options.B: Extrapancreatic gastrinomas are common, and exploration should include careful assessment of the duodenum and peripancreatic lymph nodes.
options.C: Diarrhea may be a prominent presenting feature of some patients with gastrinoma.
options.D: Before elective operation acid-reducing medications such as omeprazole should be administered. Answer: ABCD

question: Pancreas divisum results from incomplete fusion of the ventral pancreatic duct with the dorsal pancreatic duct during embryologic development. Which of the following statements correctly describes pancreas divisum?

options.A: The body and tail of the pancreas drain via an accessory ampulla distal to the ampulla of Vater. The uncinate process drains via the ampulla of Vater
options.B: The entire pancreatic ductal system drains via the ampulla of Vater
options.C: The entire pancreatic ductal system drains via an accessory ampulla proximal to the ampulla of Vater
options.D: The body and tail of the pancreas are absent. The uncinate process drains via the ampulla of Vater

question: The following statement(s) is/are true concerning the widely accepted French or Couinaud’s nomenclature for liver anatomy.

options.A: The liver is divided into eight discrete segments based on portal pedicle branches and hepatic venous drainage
options.B: This anatomy is particularly useful in allowing less than lobar segmental anatomical resections that minimize blood loss and loss of hepatic reserve
options.C: Enumeration of the system begins from right to left
options.D: Segments II and III are synonymous with the left lateral segment based on English nomenclature

question: The following statement(s) is/are true concerning hepatic anatomical nomenclature.

options.A: In the traditional English system, the right lobe is divided into anterior and posterior segments by an intersegmental line with no topographic landmarks or interparenchymal septi
options.B: The caudate lobe in the French or Couinaud’s nomenclature is referred to as segment I
options.C: The right lobe of the liver by English nomenclature is subdivided in the French system into segments V–VIII
options.D: In the English system, the left lobe of the liver is divided into the medial segment and lateral segment by the falciform ligament

question: A solitary 6 cm lesion is identified in the right hepatic lobe in the patient described above. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the initial operative management?

options.A: To facilitate mobilization and assessment with intraoperative ultrasound, complete mobilization including dividing the left and right triangular ligaments would be necessary
options.B: In dividing the right triangular ligament, care must be taken to avoid injury to accessory right hepatic veins draining directly into the vena cava
options.C: Unless a considerable length of hepatic vein is found outside the hepatic parenchyma, early hepatic vein ligation should be avoided
options.D: Ligation of the portal arterial structures is always necessary before proceeding with hepatic lobectomy

question: The liver plays a vital role in carbohydrate metabolism and regulation of blood glucose. The following statement(s) is/are true concerning carbohydrate metabolism by the liver.

options.A: Glycogen, a complex polymer of glucose, is synthesized by the hepatocyte in a remarkably energy efficient process
options.B: Glucagon stimulates glycogenesis
options.C: Glycolysis, the process by which glucose is converted to two molecules of pyruvate, occurs in the liver mitochondria
options.D: If glycogen stores become depleted, the liver is capable of synthesizing new glucose by the process of gluconeogenesis, which is stimulated by insulin

question: Transport of substances from the blood into the hepatocyte occurs through the sinusoidal membrane. The following statement(s) is/are true concerning this plasma membrane.

options.A: The high lipid content of this phospholipid bilayer allows lipid-soluble molecules to enter the cell by simple diffusion
options.B: Carrier proteins within the phospholipid bilayer bind to a solute in blood and by conformational change allow it to be transported into the cell
options.C: Large glycoprotein molecules of the sinusoidal membrane known as receptors always transport the binding ligand into the cell
options.D: The transmission of a signal to the interior of the cell by receptor-ligand binding which generates intracellular second messengers is known as signal transduction

question: The liver is an important site of protein metabolism. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning protein metabolism by the liver?

options.A: Amino acids are taken up by hepatocytes by active transport mechanisms and are generally stored long-term for later synthetic activity
options.B: Under certain conditions the amine group is removed from the amino acids in the liver and the carbon chain used for carbohydrate, lipid, or nonessential amino acid synthesis
options.C: The most important route of detoxification of ammonia formed as the result of deamination of amino acids is via excretion of ammonia into the urine
options.D: Proteins synthesized by the liver include albumin, transferrin, fibrinogen, and apolipoproteins e.Albumin is a sensitive indicator of hepatic synthetic function

question: The following statement(s) is/are true concerning hepatic blood flow.

options.A: Glucose-6-phosphate (G6P) b.Acetyl CoA
options.B: Significant hepatic storage of triglyceride or fatty infiltration can cause hepatic fibrosis or necrosis
options.C: Approximately 90% of cholesterol synthesis occurs in the liver
options.D: Most cells in the body are capable of phospholipid synthesis, therefore the liver plays a minimal role in this process

question: 105.A patient is found to develop evidence of hepatitis approximately eight weeks after receiving blood transfusions during a surgical procedure. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true?

options.A: The virus responsible is most likely hepatitis C
options.B: A chronic carrier state will ultimately develop in most patients
options.C: There is no role for interferon in the treatment of chronic hepatitis C viral infection
options.D: Chronic infection with hepatitis C is not associated with an increased risk of developing hepatocellular carcinoma

question: 108.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning treatment of pyogenic liver abscess?

options.A: Antibiotic therapy alone may be advisable in patients with multiple small abscesses
options.B: Higher grades of encephalopathy are associated with a worse prognosis c.Hypoglycemia is a common complication of all liver diseases
options.C: Sufficient antibiotic coverage for most hepatic abscesses includes coverage for gram-positive aerobic bacteria only
options.D: Liver transplantation would appear indicated in all patients with hepatic coma secondary to acute liver failure

question: 111.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the morphologic and histologic findings of cirrhosis?

options.A: Spontaneous bacterial peritonitis is an insignificant complication
options.B: Large volume paracentesis is unsafe due to excessive volume loss from the intervascular space c.Peritoneovenous shunting is a trivial surgical procedure with minimal perioperative morbidity and mortality
options.C: Bile leaks caused by rupture of bile ducts with extravasation of bile into portal triads is a common finding in post-necrotic cirrhosis secondary to hepatitis
options.D: Transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunts (TIPS) can effectively treat ascites in patients refractory to conventional medical therapy

question: 113.Which of these statement(s) is/are true concerning the etiologic factors in the development of cirrhosis?

options.A: The hemorrhoidal veins b.Left renal vein
options.B: Acetaminophen can cause acute liver failure and necrosis but will not lead to cirrhosis c.Alcohol exerts toxic effects on the liver via reactive intermediates such as acetaldehyde
options.C: The paraumbilical venous plexus
options.D: Long-standing congestive heart failure can lead to cirrhosis secondary to centrilobular congestion, hemorrhage, and necrosis 114.Important spontaneous portosystemic collaterals which develop in the face of portal hypertension include:

question: 117.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the management of gastroesophageal variceal hemorrhage?

options.A: A side-to-side portacaval shunt may be associated with the development of hepatofugal blood flow
options.B: Selective shunts preserve prograde (hepatopedal) blood flow while decompressing esophageal varices or reducing portal pressure
options.C: The presence of intractable ascites is a contraindication to the Warren shunt
options.D: If the patient is considered a liver transplant patient, an interposition mesocaval shunt is a suitable alternative

question: 128.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the treatment and prognosis of hepatocellular carcinoma?

options.A: The common hepatic and common bile duct lie immediately anterior to the portal vein
options.B: The cystic artery, which usually arises from the right hepatic artery, crosses behind the hepatic duct in most cases
