question: 131.A 35-year-old female presents with typical biliary colic symptoms, however her sonogram shows no gallstones. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning her diagnoses?

options.A: The gallbladder lies between the right, left, and quadrate hepatic lobes or hepatic segments IV and V
options.B: The cystic duct contains the spiral valve of Heister which serves an important valvular function for the gallbladder
options.C: The cystic artery arises from the right hepatic artery in 95% of cases
options.D: Gallbladder emptying during fasting is associated with phase III of the interdigestive migrating motor complex (MMC)

question: 135.The gallbladder plays an important role in altering bile composition by absorption and secretion. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning this mucosal function?

options.A: The sphincter’s basal resting pressure is 10 to 15 mm Hg above duodenal pressure b.Contraction of the sphincter occurs with CCK stimulation
options.B: Gallbladder mucosal absorption can occur by both active and passive mechanisms
options.C: Vagal stimulation results in relaxation of the sphincter
options.D: Manometry of the sphincter of Oddi may be performed at the time of ERCP to characterize basal pressure, amplitude, frequency of contraction, and direction of propagation of contractile waves

question: 139.Laparoscopic cholecystectomy has become the procedure of choice for the management of symptomatic gallstones. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning laparoscopic cholecystectomy?

options.A: Cholesterol supersaturation b.Gallbladder stasis c.Accelerated nucleation
options.B: Primary surgical management consists of relief of obstruction and cholecystectomy c.Gallstone ileus accounts for less than 5% of all causes of intestinal obstruction 141.The pathogenesis of cholesterol gallstones is multifactorial. A number of key processes, however, appear to interact closely in the formation of cholesterol gallstones. These include:
options.C: Bile duct injuries are more likely to occur during the surgeon’s early operative experience with the procedure
options.D: Previous upperabdominalsurgeryisanabsolutecontraindicationtolaparoscopic cholecystectomy 140.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning gallstone ileus? a.The diagnosis may be suggested by plain abdominal radiograph

question: 147.In which of the following clinical situations is pigment rather than cholesterol gallstones a more common problem?

options.A: Alcoholic cirrhosis b.Morbid obesity
options.B: Lymphatic drainage of the gallbladder can extend from the cystic duct lymph node to periportal lymph nodes and the celiac and superior mesenteric lymph nodes
options.C: Gallbladder cancers rarely extend past the cystic duct
options.D: Direct extension to the liver, particularly liver segments IV and V, is the most common mode of spread for gallbladder cancer

question: 149.A 55-year-old woman undergoes cholecystectomy for symptomatic gallstones. The final pathology returns one week after the operation showing gallbladder carcinoma. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning this patient’s prognosis and management?

options.A: If the gallbladder carcinoma is limited to the mucosa and/or submucosa, cholecystectomy alone is adequate treatment
options.B: If the carcinoma involves deeper layers of the gallbladder wall, the prognosis will be significantly worse
options.C: If reoperation is performed, the chance of finding residual disease will be low
options.D: If a full thickness tumor had been recognized at the time of cholecystectomy, an "extended cholecystectomy" may have been appropriate

question: 150.Gallbladder cancer accounts for 3% to 4% of all gastrointestinal tumors. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the incidence of gallbladder cancer?

options.A: Gallbladder cancer is much more frequent in men
options.B: Five year survival rates approach 50%
options.C: The combination of postoperative adjuvant radiation and chemotherapy have been associated with overall improved survival
options.D: The association of gallstones with gallbladder cancer is greater in patients with multiple small stones 151.The following statement(s) is/are true concerning the prognosis for gallbladder cancer. a.Average survival is in the range of one year

question: 152.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning gallbladder polyps?

options.A: Sonographic findings of a gallbladder polyp include a filling defect which does not move with change in position
options.B: Similar to gallbladder cancer, bile duct cancer is more common in females c.Unlike gallbladder cancer, there is no association of bile duct cancer and gallstones d.Ulcerative colitis is associated with an increased incidence of bile duct cancer
options.C: Regardless of the surgical resection for proximal biliary tumors, stenting of the biliary anastomosis is important
options.D: Complete hepatic resection with hepatic transplantation has been associated with overall good results 154.The following statement(s) is/are true concerning carcinoma of the bile ducts. a.Carcinoma of the bile ducts is more common than gallbladder carcinoma

question: 155.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the association of gallstones with gallbladder cancer?

options.A: The association of gallbladder cancer with gallstones is such that young patients with asymptomatic gallstones should undergo routine cholecystectomy
options.B: Calcification of the wall of the gallbladder is associated with an increased incidence of gallbladder cancer
options.C: Larger gallstones are associated with a greater risk of gallbladder cancer than smaller gallstones
options.D: The 20 year risk of developing gallbladder carcinoma in patients in the general population with gallstones is approximately 1%

question: 159.Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the incidence of bile duct injury following cholecystectomy?

options.A: Data from the pre-laparoscopic cholecystectomy era would suggest the incidence of bile duct injury during open cholecystectomy to be 0.1–0.2%
options.B: The current incidence of bile duct injury during laparoscopic cholecystectomy is greater than 1%
options.C: The experience of the surgeon performing laparoscopic cholecystectomy can be correlated with the incidence of bile duct injury
options.D: Intraoperative cholangiography during laparoscopic cholecystectomy will prevent bile duct injury in virtually all cases

question: 160.Primary sclerosing cholangitis has a number of treatment options—both medical and surgical. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true?

options.A: Surgical reconstruction is the only option for management of this patient
options.B: Excellent long-term results can be expected in approximately 80% of patients following surgical biliary reconstruction
options.C: One year follow-up after successful repair is satisfactory regardless of the method of management
options.D: Surgical reconstruction offers a better chance of long-term success than either percutaneous or endoscopic dilatation

question: The following comments about immune thrombocytopenic purpura (ITP) are accurate: A. Platelet count is low.

options.A: Is most common in men in their 20s.
options.B: Is frequently cured in adults by corticosteroid administration. C. Usually requires splenectomy in children.
options.C: Antiplatelet factor is immunoglobulin G (IgG) antibody. D. Purpura is directed against a platelet-associated antigen. E. May be fatal. 6. ITP:
options.D: Is most common in the sixth decade of life.

question: Ureteral obstruction:

options.A: Is associated with hematuria.
options.B: Is associated with deterioration of renal function and rising blood urea nitrogen (BUN) and creatinine values.
options.C: Is commonly caused by a urinary tract calculus.
options.D: Usually requires open surgical relief of the obstruction.

question: 19.A 65-year-old male is diagnosed as having prostatic cancer based on transrectal biopsy of a 1 cm palpable nodule. Which of the following statement(s) are true concerning his management?

options.A: If the tumor is confined within the prostatic capsule (stage A or B), radical prostatectomy is an appropriate option
options.B: If positive lymph nodes are detected on laparoscopic pelvic lymph node dissection (stage Dl), radical prostatectomy is indicated
options.C: Radical prostatectomy is invariably associated with impotence
options.D: External beam radiation is an appropriate treatment if the tumor is confined to the prostate e.There is currently no role for orchiectomy in the management of prostatic cancer

question: 20.Extracorporeal shock wave lithotripsy (ESWL) has had a dramatic effect on the management of urinary stones. Which of the following statement(s) are true concerning shock wave lithotripsy of urinary stones?

options.A: The basic principle of lithotripsy involves the generation of shock waves which are focused fluoroscopically on the calculus and are delivered to the patient who is submersed in a water bath
options.B: If cystoscopy demonstrates a bladder carcinoma as the cause of painless hematuria, no further evaluation is necessary
options.C: Multi-focal and recurrent bladder tumors are usually treated with transurethral resection and intravesical chemotherapy
options.D: The results of treatment for locally advanced bladder tumors are similar with either radical cystectomy or radiation therapy

question: 22.The most common malignant neoplasm of the kidney is the hypernephroma or renal cell carcinoma. Which of the following statement(s) are true concerning renal neoplasms?

options.A: Renal cell carcinomas can produce a variety of hormone or hormone-like substances
options.B: Bilateral multifocal renal cell cancers can be associated with the multiple endocrine neoplasia syndrome
options.C: A ―tumor deformity‖ on IVP is diagnostic of a renal cell carcinoma
options.D: Early control of the renal pedicle is an important aspect of surgical management of renal cell carcinoma e. Patients with renal cell carcinoma in a solitary kidney will inevitably require total nephrectomy and

question: 23.A 28-year-old white male presents with asymptomatic testicular enlargement. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning his diagnosis and management?

options.A: Tumor markers, b-fetoprotein (AFP) and ك-human chorionic gonadotropin (HCG) will both be of value in the patient regardless of his ultimate tissue type
options.B: Renal failure secondary to obstructive uropathy occurs as bladder pressure rises and is eventually transmitted proximally to the renal pelvis
options.C: Hormonal treatment for BPH involves treatment with a 5 a-reductase inhibitor which blocks the conversion of testosterone to the dihydrotestosterone
options.D: Intermittent catheterization, although a temporizing measure, is not an effective treatment for relief of symptoms of BPH

question: Total interruption of the radial nerve at midarm produces specific findings on physical examination. The most complete description of the neurologic deficit includes:

options.A: Paralysis of the thumb extensors, interphalangeal joint extensors, extensor carpi radialis, and extensor carpi ulnaris.
options.B: Paralysis of the extensor carpi radialis longus and brevis, abductor pollicis longus, extensor pollicis brevis, and extensor pollicis longus.
options.C: Paralysis of the brachioradialis, extensor carpi radialis longus and brevis, extensor carpi ulnaris, thumb extensors, and metacarpophalangeal (MCP) joint extensors, and loss of cutaneous sensibility at the dorsal aspect of the thumb and index fingers.
options.D: Paralysis of the brachioradialis, extensor carpi radialis longus and brevis, radialis, thumb extensors, finger MCP joint extensors, and flexor carpi radialis, and loss of sensation in the cutaneous distribution over the dorsal aspect of thumb and index fingers.

question: Which of the following are true about intracranial tumors?

options.A: Glioblastoma multiforme. B. Ependymoma.
options.B: With few exceptions, examination of the CSF is of no value in the diagnosis of an intracranial tumor.
options.C: Meningioma.
options.D: The majority of astrocytomas can be cured surgically.

question: Which of the following statements is/are true?

options.A: Cranial osteomyelitis most frequently arises from the spread of bacteria through the bloodstream from an infection elsewhere in the body.
options.B: Subdural empyema is ordinarily treated by administration of antibiotics without the need for surgical drainage.
options.C: Bacterial meningitis may lead to the development of hydrocephalus.
options.D: A bacterial brain abscess commonly presents as a mass lesion of the brain, without systemic signs of infection such as fever or leukocytosis.

question: Which of the following statements are true?

options.A: A symptomatic cervical disc herniation usually occurs in an anterolateral or anterior direction and can be removed by a surgical approach through the front of the neck.
options.B: Cervical spondylosis represents a combination of degenerative disc disease and osteoarthritis in the cervical spine.
options.C: The joints of Luschka are the main spinal facet joints.
options.D: The term cervical myelopathy refers to pain and/or neurologic dysfunction in the distribution of one or more cervical nerve roots.

question: Which of the following statements are true?

options.A: The fascicles in a peripheral nerve divide and recombine along their course.
options.B: Neurapraxia is a type of nerve injury in which the nerve is still in continuity but individual axons are disrupted.
options.C: Recovery from neurotmesis requires surgical repair.
options.D: Axonal sprouting begins 1 to 2 months after transection of a peripheral nerve. E. The patient's age influences the rate and success of nerve regeneration. Answer: ACE

question: Which of the following statements are true?

options.A: The Hoffmann-Tinel sign localizes the level of a nerve injury.
options.B: Causalgia is a term used to denote the etiology of pain.
options.C: Secondary repair of a lacerated nerve 3 to 8 weeks after injury has several advantages.
options.D: A surgeon who finds at delayed (3 to 8 weeks) exploration that a clinically nonfunctioning nerve is in continuity should resect the injured portion of the nerve and suture together the ends.

question: Myelomeningoceles are congenital malformations of the spinal cord. Which of the following findings are not commonly associated?

options.A: Hydrocephalus.
options.B: Chiari II malformation.
options.C: A midline dorsal spinal mass easily noted at birth.
options.D: Skin, bone, and dural defects superficial to the neural placode. E. Mandatory urinary incontinence.

question: 30.A 54-year-old patient with a history of successful renal transplantation is hospitalized with a diverticular abscess. Surgical exploration and drainage of the abscess with a Hartmann’s procedure is eventually required. Although the patient’s septic appearance resolves, the patient complains of severe headache and altered mental status is observed. A grand mal seizure follows. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning this patient’s management?

options.A: An intracranial epidural abscess is the likely diagnosis
options.B: A bacterial brain abscess secondary to hematogenous spread from the pericolonic infection is the likely diagnosis
options.C: The abscess expected in this case is usually solitary
options.D: Appropriate parenteral antibiotic treatment should be sufficient in this high risk patient.

question: 33.The management of a skull fracture is highly dependent on the type and location of the fracture. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning skull fractures?

options.A: A simple nondepressed linear skull fracture is of no significant consequence
options.B: Most depressed skull fractures require surgery to elevate the depressed bone fragment regardless of neurologic status
options.C: Basal skull fractures involve the base of the calvarium and may be suggested by bruising about the eye or behind the ear
options.D: CSF rhinorrhea associated with a basal skull fracture requires prompt surgical exploration and repair of the defect

question: 34.Which one or more of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning spinal cord injuries?

options.A: Incomplete spinal cord lesions may result in the Brown-Sequard syndrome which is manifest by contralateral loss of motor function and position-vibratory sensation with ipsilateral loss of pain and temperature sensation below the level of the injury
options.B: The presence of hypotension associated with a cervical spine injury following blunt trauma would suggest invariably the presence of blood loss in association with the neurologic injury
options.C: Cervical spine malalignment can almost always be reduced by skeletal traction
options.D: An indication for early operation following spinal cord injury is neurologic deterioration in a patient with initially incomplete cord lesion

question: 35.A 48-year-old man presents with chronic back pain with radiation into the buttock, posterior thigh, and calf. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true?

options.A: In the lumbar spine, more than half of clinical problems arise from L-2 to L-3 and L-3 to L-4 intervertebral discs
options.B: Most intracranial aneurysms are congenital
options.C: Up to 20% of patients with cerebral aneurysms have multiple aneurysms
options.D: Most patients with intracranial aneurysms present with signs and symptoms of subarachnoid hemorrhage with severe headache followed by neck stiffness and photophobia

question: 37.The severity of a brain injury reflects the result of both the primary injury and resulting complications constituting the secondary injury. Which of the following statement(s) concerning brain injury is/are true?

options.A: The initial neurologic finding may be dilatation of the ipsilateral pupil
options.B: If the patient has a normal neurologic examination at the time of emergency room assessment, he can be discharged safely to home
options.C: A head computed tomography (CT) scan should be performed regardless of the current neurologic examination
options.D: The likely mechanism of injury arises from a tear of a branch of the middle meningeal artery as it courses through a grove in the skull at the area of impact

question: The closing volume is:

options.A: The volume remaining in the lung at the end of expiration below which alveolar collapse begins to occur, resulting in physiologic shunting.
options.B: Higher in young persons.
options.C: Not changed during surgery.
options.D: Relative to the oxygen content of mixed venous blood. Answer: AC

question: For which patient(s) with a pulmonary infiltrate of uncertain cause would you favor VATS over open wedge excision?

options.A: An AIDS patient with a diffuse infiltrate who is ambulatory but requires supplemental oxygen. Bronchoalveolar lavage is negative.
options.B: A 64-year-old previously healthy man with increasing shortness of breath, a diffuse infiltrate, and restrictive lung disease as shown by pulmonary function studies.
options.C: A 74-year-old diabetic woman with a rapidly progressing process throughout the right lung who is ventilator- and pressor-dependent.
options.D: A 44-year-old man with fever, left-sided infiltrate, and shortness of breath.

question: Which of the following statements about the cause and prevention of postintubation tracheal stenosis are correct?

options.A: Postintubation airway stenosis can largely be avoided by providing assisted ventilation via endotracheal tube rather than tracheostomy tube.
options.B: Postintubation tracheal stenosis at the cuff level results, more or less equally, from low blood pressure, advanced age, steroids, high intracuff pressure, sensitivity to tube materials, gas sterilization elution products, and systemic disease.
options.C: In women and smaller men large endotracheal tubes can produce lesions of the glottis and subglottis that can progress to stenosis.
options.D: Stomal stenosis is due principally to cicatricial closure of large stomas resulting from removal of a disk or segment of tracheal wall during tracheostomy.

question: The following statements are true.

options.A: A distinguishing roentgenographic appearance of lung abscess, the air-fluid level can be seen only on roentgenograms obtained in the upright or lateral decubitus position.
options.B: The fungus ball characteristic of aspergillosis can be seen roentgenographically in either the upright or recumbent position.
options.C: Actinomycosis and nocardiosis are both fungal diseases of the lung that respond to treatment with the newer azole antifungal agents.
options.D: The commonest fungal lung infection in the United States is due to Histoplasma capsulatum. Answer: ABD

question: Pneumocystis pneumonia is an opportunistic infection caused by Pneumocystis carinii. Which of the following statements are true?

options.A: P. carinii is a fungus.
options.B: Pneumocystis pneumonia is the most common opportunistic infection in patients with AIDS.
options.C: The diagnosis of Pneumocystis pneumonia depends on the demonstration of P. carinii organisms in lung tissue.
options.D: There is no effective treatment for Pneumocystis pneumonia. Answer: BC

question: Which of the following statements about spontaneous pneumothorax (PSP) is/are correct?

options.A: The risk of recurrence after resolution of the first episode of PSP or secondary spontaneous pneumothorax (SSP) is 35% to 45%.
options.B: Patients with PSP are typically tall, thin, young adult males with a history of smoking.
options.C: Secondary spontaneous pneumothorax is associated with family history in 10% of cases.
options.D: For bleb resection and pleurodesis thoracoscopic thoracotomy and open thoracotomy provide similar cure rates for patients with primary spontaneous pneumothorax.

question: A solitary pulmonary nodule is discovered in an asymptomatic 55-year-old smoker with no evidence of extrathoracic dissemination. The most appropriate management would be to:

options.A: Obtain serial chest films every 3 months to determine the growth potential of the nodule.
options.B: Perform transthoracic needle aspiration (TTNA) before considering pulmonary resection to confirm malignancy.
options.C: Conduct an extensive systematic evaluation to exclude the possibility that the nodule represents a metastatic lesion.
options.D: Proceed with pulmonary resection after ascertaining that the patient would tolerate removal of the requisite amount of lung.

question: Venovenous ECMO:

options.A: Avoids major arterial access.
options.B: Provides cardiac and pulmonary support.
options.C: Can be accomplished via cannulation at separate venous sites or at a single venous site using a double-lumen catheter.
options.D: Provides greater venous drainage than venoarterial ECMO.

question: All of the following are true of the treatment of myasthenia gravis except:

options.A: The transcervical approach to surgical thymectomy is less likely to benefit the patient with myasthenia gravis.
options.B: Corticosteroids result in improvement in 80% of patients.
options.C: Plasma exchange is associated with improvement in up to 90% of patients.
options.D: Medical therapy with Mestinon (pyridostigmine) is associated with remission in approximately 10% of patients.

question: Which of the following statements about cardiac tamponade is/are correct?

options.A: At least 500 ml. of fluid must be present in the pericardium of an adult to cause symptoms of tamponade.
options.B: A drop in systemic blood pressure of greater than 20 mm. Hg during inspiration (pulsus paradoxus) is a finding specific to cardiac tamponade.
options.C: The vast majority of patients with cardiac tamponade demonstrate a low QRS voltage, nonspecific ST T-wave abnormalities, and electrical alternans (alternation of QRS amplitude) on the electrocardiogram.
options.D: In trauma victims with cardiac tamponade, the three components of ―Beck's triad‖ (hypotension, elevated jugular venous pressure (JVP), and muffled heart sounds) are almost always present.

question: Which of the following statements about constrictive pericarditis is/are correct?

options.A: The patient is inoperable due to tumor size and chest wall involvement b.Radiation therapy is the preferred initial treatment
options.B: Of all lung cancers, approximately 80% are non-small cell and 20% are small cell c.Both cell types are predictably responsive to chemotherapy
options.C: Operative resection should be performed with en bloc removal of the tumor and adjacent chest wall as well as a mediastinal lymph node resection
options.D: Positive mediastinal nodes will have little effect on survival e.The patient would be classified Stage IIIa

question: 75.A 42-year-old man has a solitary ―coin lesion‖ 2 cm in diameter in the area of the right upper lobe on a routine chest radiograph. Which of the following is/are true?

options.A: The findings suggest a parapneumonic empyema
options.B: If pus is found on aspiration of the pleural space, a chest tube should be placed
options.C: If pus is found on aspiration, bronchoscopy is a necessary part of the patient’s evaluation d.In this situation, rib resection for drainage is preferred to a large-bore chest tube
options.D: Operative treatment of a tracheoinnominate fistula includes resection and prosthetic replacement of the innominate artery 81.A 52-year-old alcoholic with fever and a cough productive of purulent sputum is found to have the opacity on chest film as shown (Fig. 62-15). The following is/are true statement(s):

question: 82.The lesion shown (Fig. 62-6) was found on a 32-year-old male on a routine chest film required for his employment. Which of the following is/are true?

options.A: The location of the lesion suggests a teratoma
options.B: High urinary vanillylmandelic acid levels would indicate that the lesion is a paraganglioma c.If the lesion was seen on a film 5 years earlier, resection would not be indicated
options.C: Mediastinoscopy for diagnosis is contraindicated
options.D: A neurosurgical consultation should be obtained Transplantation

question: Which of the following statements correctly characterize the genetic basis of histocompatibility?

options.A: Histocompatibility is determined by a series of genes inherited as a complex and subject to the mendelian rules that characterize recessive traits.
options.B: Histocompatibility depends in part on the inheritance of histocompatibility genes and in part on the inheritance of T-cell receptor genes.
options.C: Major histocompatibility genes are polymorphic.
options.D: Histocompatibility genes are independently segregating and co-dominant. E. Histocompatibility is learned. Answer: CDE

question: Which of the following distinguish MHC class I from MHC class II antigens?

options.A: MHC class I and class II antigens are encoded in different regions of the MHC complex.
options.B: MHC class I antigens are expressed on specialized antigen-presenting cells, whereas MHC class II antigens are expressed on all cells.
options.C: MHC class I and class II are members of different supergene families.
options.D: MHC class I are considered to be the major histocompatibility antigens and MHC class II the minor histocompatibility antigens.

question: Which of the following statements about immunology in liver transplantation is/are correct?

options.A: Good human leukocyte antigen (HLA) matching between recipient and donor is mandatory for a good outcome for liver transplantation.
options.B: Hyperacute rejection is almost nonexistent following liver transplantation.
options.C: Acute rejection occurs in more than 50% of patients and is reversible in most patients with large doses of steroids.
options.D: Acute rejection is very rare later than 2 months after liver transplantation unless the patient is inadequately immunosuppressed.

question: For which of the following clinical scenarios would cardiac transplantation be an appropriate therapeutic modality?

options.A: A 50-year-old man with angina pectoris, three-vessel coronary artery disease, and a left ventricular ejection fraction of 25%.
options.B: A 75-year-old woman with irremediable heart failure secondary to critical aortic stenosis.
options.C: A 25-year-old male athlete with insidious onset of heart failure secondary to idiopathic dilated cardiomyopathy.
options.D: A 55-year-old woman who is status post two previous surgeries for coronary artery revascularization, now presenting with heart failure in the absence of angina, left ventricular ejection fraction of 15%, and insufficient target coronary arteries for a third bypass procedure.

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning changes in physiology following lung transplant?

options.A: In patients with pulmonary hypertension, changes in right ventricular function and pulmonary artery pressure takes weeks to months to resolve
options.B: In single lung transplantation, changes in pulmonary function are seen almost immediately following transplantation
options.C: Patients with double lung transplants have both better pulmonary function studies as well as better exercise capabilities
options.D: After single-lung transplant, ventilation perfusion mismatch persists and carbon dioxide retention is seen

question: Current clinical protocols determine a limited number of variables and parameters for matching and allocation of donor organs to potential recipients. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning aspects of immunity important for clinical transplantation?

options.A: HLA matching is important for kidney, pancreas, and liver transplantation
options.B: A cross match assay determines if there are preformed antibodies in the recipient’s serum which will react with antigens on the cell surface of the potential donor’s lymphocytes
options.C: A patient with a history of multiple transfusions or previous transplant will have a high panel reactive antibody (PRA)
options.D: A normal heterozygous individual with a complete donor-recipient match will have a four-antigen match

question: Which of the following statements about diagnostic peritoneal lavage (DPL) is/are false?

options.A: DPL is the diagnostic procedure of choice for gunshot wounds to the abdomen with no obvious intra-abdominal injuries.
options.B: The average reported incidence of false-positive DPL in patients with significant pelvic fractures is 20% to 30%.
options.C: Accuracy rates for DPL have generally been reported between 95% and 97%.
options.D: DPL has been entirely replaced by computed tomography as the diagnostic procedure of choice following blunt abdominal trauma. Answer: AD

question: Which of the following statements or descriptions typically characterizes the syndrome of overwhelming postsplenectomy sepsis?

options.A: A syndrome of fulminant gram-negative bacteremia and septicemia in asplenic individuals, characterized by the presence of as many as 10 6 bacterial organisms per cu. mm. circulating in the bloodstream.
options.B: A syndrome caused primarily by impaired host ability to mount an effective humoral (immunoglobulin) response to infection.
options.C: A syndrome that occurs in 5% to 7% of patients following traumatic splenectomy.
options.D: A syndrome of rapidly appearing septic shock unresponsive to antibiotic therapy, with an average mortality of 50%.

question: Trauma deaths most commonly occur at three distinct time periods after injury. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the time pattern of trauma mortality?

options.A: Only 10% of trauma deaths occur within seconds or minutes of the injury
options.B: A second mortality peak occurs within hours of injury with deaths in this time period being markedly reduced with the development of trauma and rapid transport systems
options.C: Death one day to weeks after the injury are almost entirely due to infection and multiple organ failure
options.D: Late mortality in trauma patients, occurring days to weeks after the injury, has not been affected by better trauma delivery systems

question: There are a number of options for resuscitative fluids. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning fluids used for resuscitation of shock?

options.A: Resuscitation with crystalloid requires volume replacement in a ratio of 1:1 to volume lost
options.B: The literature strongly supports the use of colloid as being superior to crystalloid in the resuscitation of shock
options.C: Risks of autotransfused blood include disseminated intravascular coagulation and activation of fibrinolysis
options.D: Hypertonic saline solution results in volume expansion, an increase in left ventricular performance, decreased peripheral resistance, and redistribution of cardiac output to kidneys and viscera

question: Penetrating injuries to the pancreas and duodenum are uncommon occurring in 4% and 6% of patients, respectively. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the management of pancreaticoduodenal injuries?

options.A: The Kocher maneuver is essential for providing exposure for the duodenum
options.B: A large injury of the duodenum which cannot be closed primarily will always require a pancreaticoduodenectomy
options.C: Pyloric exclusion involves suture or staple closure of the pylorus, gastrojejunostomy, tube decompression of the duodenum, and placement of a T-tube in the common bile duct
options.D: Class III injuries of the head of the pancreas should be treated with simple external drainage rather than resection

question: The anterior neck is divided into three zones defined by horizontal planes. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning penetrating injuries to the anterior neck?

options.A: Penetrating injuries to Zone I carry the highest mortality
options.B: Injuries to Zone II are the most common and the mortality rate is second only to those of Zone I
options.C: Exposure of Zone III for detection of injuries to the distal carotid artery and pharynx can be quite difficult
options.D: All hemodynamically stable patients with penetrating injuries to Zone I should have angiography e.Most vascular lesions in Zone III are best treated by surgical exploration

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the injury pattern in patients with blunt versus penetrating injuries?

options.A: Solid organs are most frequently injured following blunt trauma
options.B: The liver is the most frequently injured organ in both penetrating and blunt trauma
options.C: Major vascular injuries occur much more commonly in penetrating trauma than with blunt abdominal trauma
options.D: Injury patterns for blunt abdominal trauma in children are different than adults whereas with penetrating trauma no such difference exists

question: An 18-year-old male suffers a gunshot wound to the abdomen, resulting in multiple injuries to the small bowel and colon. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning this patient’s perioperative management?

options.A: A multi-agent antibiotic regimen is indicated
options.B: Antibiotics should be continued postoperatively for at least 7 days
options.C: Laparotomy, as a diagnostic test for postoperative sepsis, should be considered
options.D: The incidence of postoperative wound or intraabdominal infection would be increased in association with a colon injury

question: A middle-aged man is undergoing laparotomy for blunt abdominal trauma. The spleen and liver are both found to be injured. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the management of these injuries?

options.A: If the patient has multiple other abdominal injuries and hypotension, splenic salvage should not be attempted
options.B: The incidence of life-threatening sepsis in the adult following splenectomy is no greater than in the normal population
options.C: All liver injuries regardless of their depth require external drainage
options.D: The Pringle maneuver should control all bleeding from hepatic parenchymal vessels

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct concerning the pathophysiology of frostbite?

options.A: Frostbite injury may have two components: initial freeze injury and a reperfusion injury that follows during rewarming
options.B: The formation of extracellular ice crystals in the tissue begins to occur at -10°C
options.C: The release of oxygen free radicals and arachidonic acid metabolites aggravates vasoconstriction and platelet and leukocyte aggregation
options.D: Experimental evidence suggests that a substantial component of severe cold injury may be mediated due to platelet aggregation

question: Which of the following statement(s) concerning the operative approach to abdominal trauma is/are correct?

options.A: Pelvic hematomas associated with pelvic fractures should be explored
options.B: Central retroperitoneal hematomas should be explored after control of other injuries within the peritoneal cavity
options.C: Stable hematomas in the perinephric space lateral to the midline should be explored to rule out renal injury
options.D: The initial approach is control of hemorrhage by packing and controlling ongoing contamination from enteric injuries

question: A number of systems have been developed in an effort to allow comparison of trauma injuries and trauma patients among institutions. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning trauma scoring systems?

options.A: The Revised Trauma Score uses the physiologic parameters of blood pressure, heart rate, and head injury to mathematically assess injury severity
options.B: The Abbreviated Injury Scale (AIS) is a specific anatomic index
options.C: The Injury Severity Score (ISS) correlates not only the severity of the injury but adjusts for patient age and comorbid risk factors
options.D: The Triss System is the most complete system in combining trauma score and anatomic component as well as patient age

question: Genitourinary injuries are common with both blunt and penetrating trauma. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning genitourinary trauma injuries?

options.A: All patients with microscopic hematuria and blunt trauma should be evaluated with an intravenous pyelogram
options.B: The indications for radiographic assessment of renal injury in the face of blunt trauma is more liberal than penetrating trauma
options.C: CT scan is the current imaging technique of choice for suspected renal trauma
options.D: Perinephric hematomas occurring after either penetrating or blunt trauma should not be explored

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the diagnosis and management of pelvic fractures secondary to blunt trauma?

options.A: Most pelvic fractures are apparent on the basis of physical examination
options.B: An infra-umbilical approach to peritoneal lavage in a patient with a major pelvic fracture may yield a false-positive rate approaching 50%
options.C: If a large expanding pelvic hematoma is found at surgery, the intraabdominal injury should be dealt with, and the hematoma explored
options.D: The application of pelvic external fixation may be used as the initial step in control of hemorrhage from pelvic fractures

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning the Advanced Trauma Life Support (ATLS) classification system of hemorrhagic shock?

options.A: Class I shock is equivalent to voluntary blood donation
options.B: In Class II shock there will be evidence of change in vital signs with tachycardia, tachypnea and a significant decrease in systolic blood pressure
options.C: Class III hemorrhage can usually be managed by simple administration of crystalloid solution
options.D: Class IV hemorrhage involves loss of over 40% of blood volume loss and can be classified as life-threatening

question: Which of the following is/are true about inhalation injury in burn patients?

options.A: A chest x-ray obtained within 24 hours of injury is an accurate means of diagnosis.
options.B: Its presence characteristically necessitates administration of resuscitation fluids in excess of estimated volume.
options.C: When moderate or severe, it exerts a comorbid effect that is related to both extent of burn and the age of the patient.
options.D: It increases the prevalence of bronchopneumonia.

question: Adequacy of fluid resuscitation in burn patients is indicated by which of the following?

options.A: Urine output of 45 ml. per hr. in a 70-kg. 30-year-old man with flame burns involving 55% of the total body surface.
options.B: Hourly urine output of 7 ml. in a 7-kg. 15-month-old child with burns involving 40% of the total body surface.
options.C: A pulmonary capillary wedge pressure of 17 to 20 mm. Hg.
options.D: Hourly output of 40 ml. of port wine–colored urine in an 80-kg. male who has severe high-voltage electric injury of the right arm and left leg.

question: Common electrolyte changes during and after resuscitation in a patient with a burn of 65% of the total body surface include:

options.A: A serum sodium concentration of 128 mEq. per liter following 48 hours of resuscitation fluid therapy.
options.B: A serum sodium concentration of 152 mEq. per liter on the fifth postburn day in a 75-kg. male with a 75% burn who has received only calculated maintenance fluids each day following successful resuscitation.
options.C: A serum potassium concentration of 5.7 mEq. per liter as a consequence of the destruction of red cells and other tissues in a patient with high-voltage electrical injury.
options.D: Hypokalemia due to the kaliuretic effect of 0.5% silver nitrate soaks.

question: Therapeutic interventions needed for specific chemical agents include which of the following?

options.A: Prolonged saline irrigation of eyes injured by concentrated sodium hydroxide using a scleral lens with an irrigating sidearm.
options.B: Administration of an emetic agent as immediate treatment following lye ingestion.
options.C: Intra-arterial infusion of calcium gluconate for relief of refractory deep tissue pain due to hydrofluoric acid injury.
options.D: Use of propylene glycol to remove residual phenol following water lavage.

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning topical antimicrobials in common use in the United States today?

options.A: Of the common topical antimicrobials, only mafenide acetate is painful upon application
options.B: The use of 0.5% silver nitrate is associated with trans-eschar leeching of sodium and potassium from the wound
options.C: Silver sulfadiazine has the best eschar penetration
options.D: Silver sulfadiazine, mafenide acetate, and 0.5% silver nitrate all have a broad spectrum activity, however, only silver nitrate has anti-fungal activity

question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are true concerning carbon monoxide and cyanide exposure?

options.A: A normal oxygen saturation by standard transmission pulse oximetry precludes the possibility of significant carboxyhemoglobinemia
options.B: Most patients with cyanide exposure require administration of sodium thiosulfate
options.C: The half-life of carbon monoxide is reduced by a factor of 5 with ventilation with 100% oxygen
options.D: Even if fire victims are well ventilated with high concentrations of oxygen by emergency response personnel from the time of extrication, carboxyhemoglobin values are frequently greater than 10% on initial evaluation

question: Which of the following statements describes an ideal tumor marker?

options.A: The ideal tumor marker should be tumor specific; that is, in the normal population or patients with benign diseases, false-positive test results are rare.

question: A 76-year-old female comes to the preadmission clinic for anesthetic evaluation prior to a right total hip replacement (THR) scheduled in 2 weeks. Her medical history is significant for coronary artery disease (status post stent placement 6 months ago) and baseline unstable angina one to two times per month. The patient indicates that her symptoms are relieved by sublingual nitroglycerin. A recent echocardiogram (30 days prior) showed an ejection fraction of 30% along with evidence of inferior-wall-motion abnormality. Examination of the most current EKG shows diffuse T-wave inversions with a heart rate of 60 to 65 bpm (on metoprolol) and a blood pressure of 125/60 mm Hg. In addition, the patient has severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, is dependent upon 2 L/min home O2, and has obstructive sleep apnea (on bi-level positive-airway pressure at night). options.D: Maintain hemodynamic stability during THR surgery

question: hypertrophy with bladder neck obstruction?

options.B: It is best diagnosed by endoscopy and urodynamic studies

options.D: It always results in a residual urine volume of >100ml Urethroscopy is not recommended to determine the need or treatment because the linkage between the endoscopic appearance of the LUT and the treatment outcome is poorly documented and available information suggests that the relationship is minimal. The clinical progression of LUTS associated with BPH is highly variable. Although individual studies vary, in general, about one-third of affected men have moderate symptoms that ultimately require treatment, one-third remain stable, and one-third have some regression of symptoms in the absence of intervention. Normal men have less than 12 mL of residual urine [37], but most urologists are not concerned unless the post-void residual volume is greater than 100 to 200 mL. In addition to being a possible indicator of BPH, a large residual volume is probably associated with increased risk of infection and is a precursor to bladder decompensation. question: of the following statement is true about his disease?

options.A: It is transitional in origin
options.B: Patients are usually anemic
options.C: It is extremely radiosensitive
options.D: Usual presentation is gross hematuria Renal cell Adenocarcinoma aka RCC originate from renal cortex from proximal tubular epithelium, 80-85% of primary renal neoplasms Occurs most often in men 50-70 years of age 25% of patients are asymptomatic and have incidental finding on imaging. 10% present with triad flank pain, hematuria, & mass; these are late findings and indicate advanced disease. Anemia — Anemia, which can precede the diagnosis of RCC by several months, has been reported in 29 to 88 percent of patients with advanced disease [6,9-11]. The anemia is often disproportionately severe, can be either normocytic or microcytic, and is frequently associated with iron studies typical of those observed with the anemia of chronic disease.

question: with clear cell RCC is, to some degree also associated with?

options.A: Retinal angioma

options.D: All of the above Von Hippel Lindau disease should be considered in any patient with early onset multifocal RCC or RCC in combination with any of the following history of visual or neurologic disorders, a family history of blindness, cns tumors, or renal cancer; or coexistent pancreatic cysts, epididymal lesions or inner ear tumors. The spectrum of VHL-associated tumors includes: ●Hemangioblastomas of the brain (cerebellum) and spine ●Retinal capillary hemangioblastomas (retinal angiomas) ●Clear cell renal cell carcinomas (RCCs) ●Pheochromocytomas ●Endolymphatic sac tumors of the middle ear ●Serous cystadenomas and neuroendocrine tumors of the pancreas ●Papillary cystadenomas of the epididymis and broad ligament

question: the genitourinary system?

options.A: The kidneys are approximately finger breadth superior to the iliac crest

options.C: The ureters cross the tips of the transverse processes of the lumbar vertebrae.

The trans-pyloric plane crosses through origin of SMA, body of GB, pylorus of the stomach, and through the renal hilum, the right kidney is finger breadth superior to the iliac crest. Calyces, pelvis, ureters, bladder, and urethra are lined by transitional epithelium ie urothelium In the retroperitoneum the ureter is situated just lateral to the tips of the transverse processes off the lumbar vertebrae, the ureters occupy a sagittal plane that intersects the tips off the transverse process off these lumbar vertebrae.

In UUO vasculature of kidney attempts to compensate for decreased GFR with an increase In RBF mediated by release of vasodilators PGE2, NO BUO is characterized by only a modest initial increase in RBF lasting 90 minutes followed by decrease in bilateral RB, unlike UUO in which uretral pressure is initially elevated but quickly decreases to pre-occlusion pressures by 24 hours uretral pressure remains elevated for at least 24 hours BUO Atrial natriuretic peptide appears to have unique role in BUO and may be largely responsible for the different hemodynamic response observed in BUO as compared to UUO

question: Which of the following statements is false about ureteric stones?

options.A: Mid ureteric stones are the most difficult to treat with ESWL
options.B: Nifidipine can facilitate stone passage
options.C: Size of the stone is the most important predictor for spontaneous stone passage
options.D: None of the above MET with alpha blockers was found to be beneficial while calcium antagonists was found not to be as beneficial Met is recommended for ureteric stones <10 mm Because of the bony pelvis and sacrum as well as the lack of easily identified sonographic landmarks, SWL of mid-ureteral calculi using second generation ultrasound-guided lithotriptors has presented significant difficulty. The likelihood of spontaneous passage of a ureteral stone is related to both stone size and location. Most stones ≤4 mm in diameter pass spontaneously. For stones larger than 4 mm in diameter, there is a progressive decrease in the spontaneous passage rate, which is unlikely with stones ≥10 mm in diameter. Proximal ureteral stones are also less likely to pass spontaneously.

question: A 2 year old has a right side impalpable testis but normal left testis. Which of the following statement is true?

options.A: There is greater than 90% chance of finding the testis and or cord structures at right inguinal exploration
options.B: There is greater than 10% chance of spontaneous decent of right testis in 12 months.
options.C: Orchidopexy will significantly improves the function of the right testis
options.D: Orchidopexy will reduce the frequency of malignancy occurring in the right testis Men with a history of undescended testes have an increased incidence of infertility. It is associated with lower sperm counts, sperm of poorer quality, and lower fertility rates than those of men with normally descended testes [83]. Intraabdominal and intracanalicular undescended testicles are affected similarly [84], probably related to the effect of testicular temperature on spermatogenesis [85,86]. In contrast to the risk for developing testicular cancer, there is a clear advantage to performing early orchiopexy for protection of fertility. Men with a history of undescended testis have an increased incidence of developing testicular germ cell cancers of 1 in 1000 to 1 in 2500, versus 1 in 100,000 in the general population [63-65]. Approximately 10 percent of testicular tumors occur in patients with a history of undescended testicle [66]. The risk of developing testicular cancer is affected by the location of the undescended testicle. Compared with intraabdominal testes, inguinal testes are four times less likely to become cancerous [65,67]. However, testicular malposition is not the only factor in the development of testicular cancer because 10 to 25 percent of these tumors occur in the contralateral, normally descended testicle [65,68]. In addition, surgical correction of malposition (orchiopexy) does not eliminate the risk of having testicular cancer [69]. Guidelines (AAP 1996 and EAU 2012) recommend that, in otherwise healthy males, orchiopexy should be performed by 12–18 months of age, as the gonad is unlikely to descend after 6–12 months of age and there is not an added benefit from waiting. In most cases, the undescended testis is located near the neck of the scrotum, just outside or a little lateral to the external inguinal ring, in the “superficial inguinal pouch,” which is the misplaced tunica vaginalis

question: Which of the following investigations is the most important to perform prior to excision of a suspected thvroglossal cyst?

options.A: US of the cyst
options.B: Chest X-ray
options.C: TFT
options.D: US of thyroid gland Patients with a TGDC often have ectopic thyroid glands. Before any surgery is planned, it is important to determine whether patients with a TGDC have thyroid tissue in the normal site. It is essential to determine whether a patient has ectopic thyroid tissue, as this can be involved with benign or malignant thyroid disease. Ectopic thyroid tissue can be confused with a TGDC. All cases of thyroid ectopia should have thyroid function tests, ultrasonography and a thyroid scan performed to locate additional functioning thyroid tissue

Urethral stricture is due to tightening of the reconstructed urethra within the glans, which is referred to as meatal stenosis. Urethral diverticulum is due to outpouching of the reconstructed urethra. Most boys with proximal hypospadias have ventral curvature (VC) which must be straightened while preserving the urethral plate to use TIP repair. That is usually done by dorsal plication (DP). However, we reported recurrent VC was commonly found after DP in boys with proximal urethroplasty complications.

options.A: Splenic infarction
options.B: Rectus sheath hematoma
